title: Incremental State Machines, Concurrency, Cancellation, and Ownership · Program Defense
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for incremental state machines, concurrency, cancellation, and ownership and requires the smallest strict explicit protocol state, event, invariant, and ownership model with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for explicit protocol state, event, invariant, and ownership model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Represent protocol phase, required input, transition, emitted event, retained buffer, limit, and terminal result explicitly across fragmented reads.
Define invariants for cursor bounds, monotonic consumption, one framing choice, one response order, one close owner, and no effects before validation.
Assign one coordinated reader and ordered writer per HTTP/1.1 connection with explicit handoff for tunnels and shutdown.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Incremental State Machine · changed case · Quiz Incremental State Machine
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz protocol team handles case 5498 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce cursor, framing, reader, and terminal ownership invariants; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
